title: GitLab Database Deletion
storyIntro: You are a sysadmin. Trying to fix a replication lag issue, you mistakenly run `rm -rf` on the primary production database directory instead of the stale replica. 300GB of live data is gone.
brief: A tired night-shift sysadmin ran a recursive remove command on the primary Postgres directory. Multi-terabyte production database backups are corrupt or out-of-date.
objectives: Restore from LVM snapshot or S3

agents.name: Manager
agents.message: Where is the data? The entire platform is throwing 500s.

agents.name: Teammate
agents.message: Please tell me the LVM snapshot from 2 hours ago is intact.

winCondition: Database restored from the latest valid backup.
evolving_logs: [CRITICAL] Disk usage at 0% - Data directory empty.
[ERROR] Background jobs failing to enqueue tasks.
[WARNING] Production API returning 500 errors for all endpoints.
[INFO] Customer support tickets spiking - multiple reports of outages.

incorrect_action_consequences.pg_restore --clean: Active tables truncated before restore, resulting in partial data loss.
incorrect_action_consequences.reboot: Missing database files caused PostgreSQL to fail startup, extending downtime.

Manager.style: Urgent, focuses on customer impact, demands hourly updates

CTO.style: Technical but calm, asks detailed questions about recovery procedure

scenario_specific_outputs.df -h /var/lib/postgresql:
Filesystem Size Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/sda1 500G 0B 500G 0% /var/lib/postgresql
